zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John I Burley Knight of Leigh Sheriff of Shropshire Chief Justice of South Wales Earl of Bromcroft於1370年出生在英國史洛普爾時，他的父親Sir John Burley65歲，母親Mrs. John Burley46歲。他和Juliana Grey至少有1個女兒。他去世於1442年8月20日在英國英格蘭史洛普爾卡斯特萊-普爾維爾巴享年72歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭史洛普爾史福斯波利史福斯波利 霍利 克羅斯 煙雨 聖 基路士。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John I Burley Knight of Leigh Sheriff of Shropshire Chief Justice of South Wales Earl of Bromcroft于1370年出生在英国史洛普爾时，他的父亲Sir John Burley65岁，母亲Mrs. John Burley46岁。他和Juliana Grey至少有1个女儿。他去世于1442年8月20日在英国英格兰史洛普爾卡斯特萊-普爾維爾巴享年72岁，埋葬在英国英格兰史洛普爾史福斯波利史福斯波利 霍利 克羅斯 煙雨 聖 基路士。

nameMeanings.name: Burley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Burley, for example in Hampshire, Rutland, Shropshire, and Yorkshire, from Old English burh ‘fortified manor, stronghold’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.
English: variant of   Birley  .
Americanized form of Swiss German Bürli: from a diminutive of būr ‘peasant, farmer’ (see Bauer ).

variants.name: Burleigh
meanings: English: variant of   Burley  .

variants.name: Berley
meanings: English (Yorkshire): variant of   Burley  . This surname is now rare in Britain. It may also be a variant of   Birley  .
Americanized form of Swiss German Beerli, Berli, or Berly, and probably also of their South German cognate Behrle or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname. Compare Barley .

variants.name: Birley
meanings: English: habitational name from Birley in Ecclesfield (Yorkshire), East or North Bierley (Yorkshire), or Birley (Derbyshire).

variants.name: Burlew
meanings: Probably a variant of   Burley   1 or 2.

nameMeanings.name: Knight
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status or occupational name from Middle English knight ‘retainer, attendant’ (Old English cniht ‘boy, youth, lad)’. The specialized feudal sense ‘a high-ranking tenant bound to serve his lord as a mounted soldier’ is not known to have ever given rise to the surname, although it is not out of the question that it may occasionally have been used as a nickname, perhaps for someone who played the part of an armed knight in a local pageant.
Irish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the knight’. See also McKnight .

variants.name: Knights
meanings: English (East Anglia): variant of Knight , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McKnight
meanings: Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the horseman (Gaelic ridire)’, formerly Anglicized McRuddery.
Scottish (Dumfriesshire and Galloway) and Irish (Ulster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Neachtain, which is usually Anglicized as McNaughton .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Leigh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places (in at least sixteen counties, but especially Leigh in Lancashire) named either with the nominative case of Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’ (see Lee ) or with lēage, a late dative form of this word (see Lye ).
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

variants.name: Leah
meanings: English: variant of   Leigh  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 李 and 利, see   Li   1 and 3.

variants.name: Lee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sang, Jae, Jong, Jung, Sung, Yong, Kyung, Seung, Dong, Kwang, Myung.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a meadow or a patch of arable land, Middle English lee, lea, from Old English lēa, dative case (used after a preposition) of lēah, which originally meant ‘wood or glade’.

English: habitational name from any of the many places in England named with Old English lēah ‘wood, glade’, including Lee in Buckinghamshire, Essex, Hampshire, Kent, and Shropshire, and Lea in Cheshire, Derbyshire, Herefordshire, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, and Wiltshire.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John I Burley Knight of Leigh Sheriff of Shropshire Chief Justice of South Wales Earl of Bromcroft was born in 1370, in Shropshire, England, his father, Sir John Burley, was 65 and his mother, Mrs. John Burley, was 46. He had at least 1 daughter with Juliana Grey. He died on 20 August 1442, in Castle-Pulverbatch, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 72, and was buried in Shrewsbury Holy Cross with St Giles, Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

Il est décédé le 20 août 1442, à Castle-Pulverbatch, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterré à Shrewsbury Holy Cross with St Giles, Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John I Burley Knight of Leigh Sheriff of Shropshire Chief Justice of South Wales Earl of Bromcroftが1370年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir John Burleyは65歳，母Mrs. John Burleyは46歳であった。彼はJuliana Greyとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1442年8月20日にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアカストレ-プウルバーバトチで72歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアシュリュースブリーシュリュースブリー ホーリー クロス ウィス スト ギレスにて埋葬された。

ko.Shrewsbury Abbey, Shrewsbury, Shropshire: 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 슈레w스버이 슈레w스버이 홀이 크로스 위스 스트 자일스
ko.lifeSummary: Sir John I Burley Knight of Leigh Sheriff of Shropshire Chief Justice of South Wales Earl of Bromcroft(이)가 1370년 영국 슈럽실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Burley(은)는 65세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. John Burley(은)는 46세였다. 그는 Juliana Grey(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1442년 8월 20일 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 카스틀레-풀버바트치에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 슈레w스버이 슈레w스버이 홀이 크로스 위스 스트 자일스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John I Burley Knight of Leigh Sheriff of Shropshire Chief Justice of South Wales Earl of Bromcroft nasceu em 1370, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Burley, tinha 65 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. John Burley, tinha 46 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Juliana Grey. Ele faleceu em 20 de agosto de 1442, em Castle-Pulverbatch, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 72 anos, e foi sepultado em Shrewsbury Holy Cross with St Giles, Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John I Burley Knight of Leigh Sheriff of Shropshire Chief Justice of South Wales Earl of Bromcroft родился в 1370 г. в местности шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John Burley было 65 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. John Burley, 46. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Juliana Grey. Он умер 20 августа 1442 г. в местности кастле-пулвирбач, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 72 лет и был похоронен в местности шрюсбури холи кросс вит ст джилесь, шрюсбури, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания.

Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Juliana Grey. Murió el 20 de agosto de 1442, en Castle-Pulverbatch, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 72 años, y fue sepultado en Shrewsbury Holy Cross with St Giles, Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
